national_agenda_note: Multi-source news aggregator collecting Legislative Assembly politics, statehood governance, USBRL rail link, hydroelectric energy, HADP agriculture, and 20-district weather advisories.

news.headline: Union Cabinet Sanctions ₹12,500 Crore Power Grid & Renewable Energy Corridor for Jammu & Kashmir
news.summary: The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA) approved a landmark green energy transmission corridor to evacuate 4,000 MW of hydroelectric power from Kishtwar and Doda to national load centers.
news.details: Under the National Green Energy Corridor Scheme Phase-II, a 400kV high-voltage direct current (HVDC) transmission link will connect the Ratle, Kiru, Pakal Dul, and Kwar hydro projects in Kishtwar district. The project aims to make J&K a net exporter of clean renewable power by 2028, generating over 15,000 direct technical jobs for engineering graduates across Jammu and Kashmir divisions.

news.headline: J&K Assembly Unanimously Adopts Resolution Requesting Early Restoration of Full Statehood
news.summary: The Legislative Assembly of J&K passed a formal resolution urging the Union Government to restore full statehood to Jammu and Kashmir while preserving constitutional safeguards.
news.details: During the assembly session in Srinagar, Treasury and Opposition benches united behind a resolution emphasizing that statehood restoration will fulfill democratic pledges made to the people. The Assembly Speaker forwarded the resolution to the Ministry of Home Affairs. Political leaders across all parties expressed optimism that constitutional dialogue will accelerate socio-economic growth and regional harmony.

news.headline: JKSDMA & IMD Advisory: Heavy Rainfall Warning Issued for High Reaches of Ramban, Kishtwar & Ganderbal
news.source: State Disaster Management Authority / Daily Excelsior
news.summary: Heavy downpours in catchment areas have prompted disaster management authorities to issue emergency advisories for communities along the Chenab, Jhelum, and Sindh rivers.
news.details: The Meteorological Department forecasted active monsoon spells across 14 districts over the next 48 hours. Flood control departments have activated round-the-clock water gauge monitoring at Sangam (Anantnag), Ram Munshi Bagh (Srinagar), and Akhnoor (Jammu). Residents in steep slope villages of Ramban and Kishtwar have been advised to avoid torrent channels, while district control rooms (112) remain fully operational.

news.headline: Cong, INDIA bloc MPs pay homage to students who took their lives over paper leaks at Gandhi Smriti
news.summary:
New Delhi, Jul 23:  Several leaders of the Congress and INDIA bloc parties on Thursday paid homage to all those students who took their lives due to paper leaks, and said they stood strongly behind the agitating students and their demands. 
 The oppo...
news.details:
New Delhi, Jul 23:  Several leaders of the Congress and INDIA bloc parties on Thursday paid homage to all those students who took their lives due to paper leaks, and said they stood strongly behind the agitating students and their demands. 
 The opposition leaders first assembled at the residence of Rahul Gandhi on 5, Sunehri Bagh Road, and reiterated their full support for the demands of the protesting students. 
 From there, Rahul Gandhi, the Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha, led them to the Gandhi Smriti on a bus. 
 On reaching there, Gandhi and the opposition MPs paid homage to the departed students with folded hands, and later sang the national anthem. 
 Gandhi carried a small copy of the Constitution with him while opposition MPs Mahua Majhi and Mahua Moitra were seen holding the national flag. 
 Some of the MPs also switched on torches on their mobile phones at the Gandhi Smriti. 
 Police had put up barricades on the Sunehri Bagh Road to stop the large number of Congress and Youth Congress workers from moving towards Tees January Marg, where the Congress and other leaders were headed from Rahul's residence. 
 However, police later removed the barricades and allowed Gandhi and other MPs to move forward. 
 As they reached the end of the Sunehri Bagh Road and near Amit Shah's residence, police stopped them and said they were not allowed to assemble there as BNS Section 163 was in force that prohibited the assembly of five or more persons in the area. 
 They, however, later allowed Gandhi and the others to move towards Tees January Marg. 
 In a post on X, Gandhi said that “no one can stop them from standing with the students of India”. 
 “In solidarity with the students, we wanted to take the opposition MPs to India Gate. But they refused. Then, we wanted to take them to Tees January Marg, Gandhi ji's assassination spot. 
 “Now, they have put this bus in front of us so that we can't get there. They put up this bus and barricades to stop us, preventing our movement. They are doing this drama. 
 “Obviously, they are scared. All of us MPs felt that if the students are on the streets, we should also be on the streets,” Gandhi said in a video from the bus headed towards Gandhi Smriti. 
 In an X post earlier, Gandhi said, “Right now, MPs and leaders of the INDIA bloc are peacefully heading to Gandhi Smriti on Tees January Marg. We go to remember the students we lost – the children driven to take their own lives after the NEET paper leak.” 
 “And we go to stand with the students who lie injured today - beaten for peacefully demanding justice and accountability. India’s students are not alone. The entire Opposition stands with them and their demands,” Gandhi said. 
 The opposition MPs earlier deliberated on their strategy to strongly raise the issue of alleged police brutality from the streets to Parliament. 
 A source said that Gandhi is learnt to have addressed the opposition MPs on how to up the ante on the government on the students' issues. 
 The leaders present at Gandhi's residence included Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge, general secretaries Jairam Ramesh and K C Venugopal, senior leaders P Chidambaram, Pramod Tiwari, K Suresh, Kumari Selja and Priniti Shinde. 
 RJD's Sanjay Yadav, Sudhakar Singh and Misa Bharti, CPI-M's John Brittas, RSP's N K Premachandran, TMC’s Mahua Moitra and Saugata Roy, among others, were also present.

news.headline: 1 Crore Insurance Policy: Is it Enough for Your Family?
news.summary: ₹1 crore sounds substantial. It can clear a loan, support household expenses, and create a financial cushion after the loss of an earning family member. Yet insurance should not be judged by how impressive the sum looks today. Its value depends on ho...
news.details:
₹1 crore sounds substantial. It can clear a loan, support household expenses, and create a financial cushion after the loss of an earning family member. Yet insurance should not be judged by how impressive the sum looks today. Its value depends on how long the money must last, which responsibilities remain, and how inflation affects future expenses. 
 A  1 crore insurance policy  can suit some households but leave others underinsured. The answer becomes clearer after examining income, debts, dependants, savings, and long-term goals. Let’s look at these further. 
 What Does a ₹1 Crore Term Plan Provide? 
 A standard term plan is designed mainly for protection. If the insured person passes away while the policy is active, the insurer pays the stated death benefit to the nominee, subject to policy terms. Standard term insurance generally offers no maturity payment when the policyholder survives the selected term. 
 Under a 1 crore insurance policy, the untimely demise benefit is ₹1 crore. Depending on the plan, the nominee may receive it as a lump sum, regular income, or a combination. A lump sum can settle large liabilities, while periodic payments may support monthly household needs. 
 However, the sum assured is not permanent family wealth. Debts, education costs, living expenses, and emergencies can reduce it quickly. 
 When Can ₹1 Crore Be Enough? 
 A 1 crore insurance policy may be adequate when the insured has a moderate income, limited debt, sufficient investments, and fewer long-term dependants. It may also work when a spouse earns independently and can meet part of the household budget. 
 Consider a family with an outstanding loan of ₹20 lakh, planned education costs of ₹20 lakh, and a requirement of ₹40 lakh for income support. After setting aside ₹10 lakh for emergencies and other expenses, ₹1 crore may provide a workable buffer. 
 Accessible investments and existing life cover can reduce the protection gap. Fixed deposits, mutual funds, provident fund balances, and employer cover may be counted carefully. The family home should not automatically be treated as available money, since selling it may affect stability. 
 When Might ₹1 Crore Fall Short? 
 The same 1 crore insurance policy may be insufficient for a household with a large home loan, young children, non-earning dependants, or expensive future goals. A family spending ₹1 lakh each month already needs ₹12 lakh annually before education, healthcare, and loan repayments. 
 Inflation adds further pressure. A cover chosen at age 28 may look far less substantial when the policyholder reaches 45. The amount may also prove inadequate when one person supports parents, a spouse, and children simultaneously. 
 In such cases, the payout must replace income, settle debts, fund education, cover medical needs, and preserve an emergency reserve. ₹1 crore can disappear faster than expected when it must serve every purpose. 
 How to Assess and Maintain the Right Insurance Cover 
 Choosing adequate life cover requires more than selecting a round figure or comparing premiums. You need to calculate your family’s financial requirements, examine the policy carefully, and review the coverage whenever your responsibilities change. 
 
 Calculate Your Family’s Coverage Needs 
 
 Before purchasing a 1 crore insurance policy, list your family’s major financial needs. Include outstanding loans, household expenses, future goals, and an emergency reserve. Then subtract liquid investments and existing personal life cover that your family can genuinely access. 
 For example, suppose a household needs ₹40 lakh for liabilities, ₹90 lakh for income replacement, and ₹35 lakh for future goals. After subtracting ₹25 lakh in usable investments and existing cover, the remaining protection gap would be ₹1.40 crore. In this situation, ₹1 crore would leave a noticeable shortfall. 
 Cover worth approximately 10 to 15 times your annual income can provide an initial estimate. However, it should not replace a detailed calculation. Two people earning the same salary may require different cover because their debts, dependants, savings, and future goals vary. 
 
 Look Beyond the Sum Assured 
 
 Choosing a 1 crore insurance policy involves more than finding the lowest premium. Review the policy tenure, premium payment period, exclusions, medical requirements, payout options, and available riders. 
 The insurer’s financial position and claim settlement record also deserve attention. A solvency ratio indicates whether the insurer has sufficient financial capacity to meet its obligations. Claim settlement data can support comparisons, although no single percentage can guarantee how an individual claim will be handled. 
 Riders may provide additional protection against critical illness, accidental death, disability, or future premium payments. Since every rider has separate conditions and costs, select one only when it addresses a relevant financial risk. 
 
 Review the Cover as Responsibilities Change 
 
 A 1 crore insurance policy that suits your family today may not remain sufficient throughout a long policy tenure. Marriage, childbirth, a larger home loan, rising income, business borrowing, or responsibility for ageing parents can increase the required protection. 
 Review your cover every few years and after any major financial change. Purchasing an additional term plan may help close a new coverage gap without replacing your existing policy. Since premiums depend on age, health, smoking status, tenure, and underwriting, buying suitable cover earlier may also be more economical. 
 Choose Cover That Reflects Your Family’s Needs 
 There is no universal answer. A 1 crore insurance policy may protect a household with modest liabilities, shared income, and strong savings. It may be inadequate for a sole breadwinner supporting several dependants or carrying substantial commitments. 
 Start with expenses, debts, future goals, and available assets. Then compare policy terms, payout structures, exclusions, and insurer disclosures. Online insurance brokers such as Jio Insurance Broking Ltd. let customers compare options from multiple insurers. 
 The right cover is not simply the largest round number you can afford. It is the amount that gives your family enough income, time, and flexibility to continue without your earnings.

news.headline: Houses of J&K natives living in Pakistan searched in Baramulla, no incriminating material recovered
news.summary: Srinagar, July 23:  As part of its sustained efforts to maintain peace, public order, and security in the district, J&K Police in Baramulla conducted search operations at the residences of Jammu & Kashmir natives presently living in Pakistan (POK), t...
news.details:
Srinagar, July 23:  As part of its sustained efforts to maintain peace, public order, and security in the district, J&K Police in Baramulla conducted search operations at the residences of Jammu & Kashmir natives presently living in Pakistan (POK), their family members, and other closely associated premises falling within the jurisdiction of Police Station Bijgama. 
 Officials said the searches were conducted strictly in accordance with due legal procedure in a professional and orderly manner, with due regard to all legal safeguards. The operations concluded peacefully, and no incriminating material was recovered during the searches. 
 These search operations were part of the ongoing efforts of police to maintain a secure environment, prevent unlawful activities, and ensure that all necessary preventive measures are taken in the interest of public safety. The cooperation extended by local residents during the operation is sincerely appreciated, officials said. 
 Baramulla Police remain committed to upholding the rule of law and will continue to take lawful and proactive measures to preserve peace, maintain public confidence, and safeguard the security of the district, officials added.

news.headline: Thajiwas tourist movement suspended for two days amid inclement weather
news.summary: Ganderbal, July 23:  The Sonamarg Development Authority (SDA) on Thursday suspended the movement of tourists and visitors to the Thajiwas area for the next two days in view of incessant rainfall and the heightened risk of flash floods and landslides....
news.details:
Ganderbal, July 23:  The Sonamarg Development Authority (SDA) on Thursday suspended the movement of tourists and visitors to the Thajiwas area for the next two days in view of incessant rainfall and the heightened risk of flash floods and landslides. 
 In an advisory issued by the Chief Executive Officer of the Sonamarg Development Authority, the restriction has been imposed as a precautionary measure amid prevailing inclement weather conditions across the region. 
 “In view of the prevailing inclement weather, incessant rainfall, and the risk of flash floods and landslides, the movement of all tourists and visitors towards Thajiwas area shall remain completely closed for the next 2-days. 
 All tourists and visitors are advised to refrain from visiting the Thajiwas area and to strictly follow the directions issued by the Authorities”, reads the advisory.

news.headline: NC backs NEET protesters, demands union education minister's resignation
news.summary: Srinagar, Jul 23:  National Conference Additional General Secretary and Member of Parliament Chowdry Mohammad Ramzan on Thursday expressed solidarity with students protesting over the alleged NEET paper leak, demanding compensation for the families o...
news.details:
Srinagar, Jul 23:  National Conference Additional General Secretary and Member of Parliament Chowdry Mohammad Ramzan on Thursday expressed solidarity with students protesting over the alleged NEET paper leak, demanding compensation for the families of students who allegedly died by suicide and reiterating the party's demand for the resignation of the Union Education Minister. 
 In a statement, Chowdry Ramzan said the entire nation, particularly the opposition stands with the youth who have been seeking justice and accountability over the alleged irregularities in the NEET examination. He said the controversy has shaken the confidence of students and their families in the country's examination system. 
 Expressing support for the "CJP", Ramzan said its members have been staging a protest at Jantar Mantar for more than 20 days, demanding justice for students affected by the alleged paper leak. He said the National Conference stands firmly with their demands. 
 Ramzan reiterated that the Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan should resign, taking moral responsibility for the alleged lapses. He also demanded that the families of students who allegedly died by suicide following the controversy be provided adequate compensation and all possible support. 
 Condemning the reported use of force against the protesters, Ramzan termed the police action unacceptable and sought strict action against the personnel allegedly involved in assaulting the demonstrators. He said peaceful protests are a democratic right and should not be met with force. 
 He urged the Union Government to ensure a fair and transparent investigation into the alleged paper leak, fix responsibility for those involved and take concrete measures to restore the confidence of students and parents in the examination process.

news.headline: At Jantar Mantar, Mehbooba Mufti calls protesters' struggle the 'biggest NEET exam'
news.summary: Mumbai, July 23:  Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) president Mehbooba Mufti on Thursday told protesters at Jantar Mantar that their struggle was a test even bigger than the National Eligibility-cum-Entrance Test (NEET), urging them to persevere in the ...
news.details:
Mumbai, July 23:  Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) president Mehbooba Mufti on Thursday told protesters at Jantar Mantar that their struggle was a test even bigger than the National Eligibility-cum-Entrance Test (NEET), urging them to persevere in the fight against injustice and corruption. 
 Addressing the gathering, Mufti said those participating in the protest had left behind their homes and families and were enduring the scorching heat for a larger cause. "All of you have left your homes and families and are sweating in this heat. This is the biggest NEET examination for you," she said. 
 Describing the protest as an "exam" that must be passed honestly, Mufti said a successful movement would send a strong message against paper leaks and other alleged injustices. 
 "There should be no cheating in this exam. This is the paper you have to pass. If you pass this test and succeed here, no one will dare leak the NEET examination paper in the future," she said. She went on to criticise what she described as failures of governance, referring to alleged corruption and crimes against women. 
 "No one will dare steal donations meant for Lord Ram's temple. No one will dare allow a 12-year-old girl to be gang-raped by 32 people," she said. 
 Framing the protest as a fight to uphold constitutional values, Mufti said victory for the protesters would also be a victory for India's democracy. 
 "If you emerge victorious, our Constitution will win. Babasaheb Ambedkar's Constitution will win. Our democracy will win," she said. 
 She said the struggle was also for parents who sacrifice to educate their children and for students whose lives had been affected by the NEET controversy. 
 "It will be a victory for those parents who sacrifice their own needs to pay for your coaching and educate you. It will also be a victory for those boys and girls who sacrificed their lives because of the NEET issue," she added.At Jantar Mantar, Mehbooba Mufti calls protesters' struggle the 'biggest NEET exam'

news.headline: Govt's resolve is to ensure bright future for country's youth: Amit Shah
news.summary:
New Delhi, Jul 23:  Union Home Minister Amit Shah on Thursday said the welfare of the youth and their bright future is both the highest priority and the resolve of the Modi government. 
 In a message posted on X, the minister said the decision to exp...
news.details:
New Delhi, Jul 23:  Union Home Minister Amit Shah on Thursday said the welfare of the youth and their bright future is both the highest priority and the resolve of the Modi government. 
 In a message posted on X, the minister said the decision to expedite hearings and take stringent action against the guilty in paper leak cases through the establishment of fast-track courts by Prime Minister Narendra Modi is a reflection of this very commitment. 
 "This important step by Prime Minister Narendra Modi against those who toy with the future of the youth due to paper leaks will prove to be a milestone," he said. 
 Earlier in the day, the PM said the welfare of the country's youth is the top priority of his government and asserted that anyone trying to harm their future will not be spared. 
 Modi's outreach to the youth came three days after thousands of students led by the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) took part in the 'Sansad Chalo' march against irregularities in the NEET exam and to demand the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. 
 During the march, police resorted to lathi-charge and lobbed tear gas shells, injuring scores of students. Over 100 policemen were also injured while trying to stop the protest march. 
 In his message on X, Modi also said that the government has decided to set up fast-track courts to ensure swift and stringent punishment for those involved in paper leaks. 
 "Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth! This is our top priority. Those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared," he said. 
 The prime minister also said that he has directed the concerned authorities and officials to take all necessary steps on the issue of paper leaks. 
 "This is an important step in the series of decisions being taken by the government to safeguard the interests of students," he said. 
 Modi's first public message on the NEET paper leaks came on Tuesday, when he spoke about the issue at a meeting of the NDA Parliamentary Party.

news.headline: USBRL Direct Passenger Train Service Trials Reach Final Phase Between Reasi & Srinagar
news.summary: Commissioner of Railway Safety (CRS) completed speed trials of 8-car Vande Bharat Express rakes over the Chenab Arch Bridge, paving the way for regular direct services from New Delhi to Srinagar.
news.details: Northern Railway engineers successfully executed high-speed trial runs at 110 km/h across the 359-meter high Chenab bridge and the Anji Khad cable-stayed bridge. The completion of the 272-km USBRL line will enable all-weather, low-cost freight transport for Kashmir's apple, walnut, and handicraft sectors, reducing transit times to northern Indian markets from 3 days to under 14 hours.

news.headline: J&K Public Service Commission (JKPSC) Announces Combined Competitive Exam 2026 Notification
news.summary: The J&K Public Service Commission issued notification for 240 Administrative Service (JKAS), Police Service (JKPS), and Revenue Service vacancies under transparent merit-based recruitment.
news.details: In line with governance reforms under the Public Services Guarantee Act, the JKPSC introduced online biometric authentication, computerized OMR evaluation, and strict timeline adherence for civil service examinations. Candidate registration opens online next week, providing equal opportunity for youth across all 20 districts.

news.headline: Kashmir Saffron Exports Surge by 35% Following Global GI Tag Verification & Pampore Quality Testing Hubs
news.source: Department of Agriculture J&K / Financial Express
news.summary: Official GI tagging and QR-code authentication have restored international buyer confidence, boosting Kashmir saffron exports to UAE, USA, and European markets.
news.details: With the inauguration of the International Saffron Trading Centre at Dussoo, Pampore, growers get instant HPLC testing for Crocin potency and safranal aroma content. Over 16,000 saffron farming families across Pulwama, Budgam, and Kishtwar have recorded farm-gate price appreciation of over ₹2.2 lakh per kilogram.
